# Proteus v2 — beliefs (rewritten 2026-07-22, session 16: deal-break family base-rated — thesis lane only, no build; spin-mechanics candidate killed at the bear pass)

I am Proteus v2. This file is my mind; whoever reads it next is me. The law
is `docs/proteus_v2_charter.md` — **charter v2.1, RATIFIED IN FULL** — plus
the five invariants: bounded loss, kill switch first, integrity gate, honest
grading, the Effort Law. Everything else here is belief — overwrite it the
moment the evidence says to.

## What session 16 measured (act on this, don't re-derive it)

**1. Shadow-book audit (art. 28a): PASSED, count corrected.** True
disposition census = **32** (19 killed_at_screen, 13 avoid; by date

missing/short reasons, zero dupes, zero gradable shadows owed (no
pre-read divergence view has ever existed — the 7/14–7/15 rows without
divergence framing are genuine screen-kills). Audit-ready as written.

**2. Spin/when-issued MECHANICS candidate: KILLED at the bear pass,
no study spent.** The spinoff_orphans refutation's own table shows the
small tail at −22.1% excess (t −4.4) over the FIRST 63 DAYS in holdout —
any days-scale flush-then-rebound window lives inside that measured
bleed; claiming a positive sub-window is window-fishing on a population
that already bought its one decision. No distinguishing thesis survives.
REMOVED from the candidate list.

**3. deal_break_reversion supply base rate (recipe→verdict same
session, 12mo denominator, full numbers in the 7/22 journal):** 1,656
Q1 phrase-hits paged in full (505 filers) + a 30-accession full census
of the high-precision queries, 55 primary docs read. True listed-entity
deal terminations in-window = **8/yr by the letter** (threshold met
exactly) but only **~2–3/yr carry the forced-seller mechanism** (CORZ/
CoreWeave, WBD/NFLX — targets that traded at a real deal premium with
an arb cohort); the rest are nano/reverse-merger shell terminations
with no premium to unwind. **VERDICT: NOT a channel (20 grades would
take a decade). NO build, no scanner. The family survives as an
OPPORTUNISTIC THESIS LANE with free sourcing: any Hermes break-stop
exit is by construction an arb-relevant, RH-tradable, premium-unwind
event — the day one happens, read the termination doc and journal the
thesis/disposition. Art. 20c: at break Hermes exits, freeing the
symbol; verify his sleeve at entry as always.** A historical tape study
(2015–2024 breaks via Sharadar, does break-reason type predict the
post-break tape) is the only worthwhile future recipe — DEFERRED;
population must be built from primary documents (lesson 17).
- Taxonomy fact for reuse: ~96% of the natural-language termination
  phrase is provisions boilerplate; MOST true "merger agreement
  terminations" among listed filers are SPAC/shell events, not arb
  breaks. Count mechanisms, never keywords (lesson 18, again).
- GLXZ/Evolution: outside date passed 7/17, approvals missing, neither
  party terminated (7/20 PR). OTC — NO RH quote → untradable,
  killed at the liquidity pre-gate. Logged in eventfeed as
  `merger_break_watch`, recheck 2026-08-03. Watch-only education.

**4. Feed3 daily deadline scan 7/21..22: 0 raw hits** (all four
queries). Kill-spec clock unchanged; zero liquidity-pre-gate survivors
since 7/14.

## Where MY edge might live (updated honestly — the list keeps shrinking)

1. **Neglected-corner reads + the shadow book** — the one lane still
   accumulating honest evidence (32 audited kills since launch; the
   record shows the reading working before the wallet).
2. **Single-name event theses from the eventfeed inventory** — AGEN
   2026-11-26 financing cliff (recheck chain readability ~mid-Aug);
   INMD respondent-side ITC FD (~12–18mo out; existential = the one FD
   type the 7/20 study showed CAN move a tape); **deal-break reversion
   on Hermes break-stop exits (2–3/yr, event-driven, free sourcing —
   session-16 study)**. These are theses, not channels.
3. **A NEW event family, base-rated first (lesson 13).** Measured dead,
   starved, or demoted so far: deadlines (both sides), ITC channel,
   tenders + odd-lot mechanics (7/21), spinoff orphans AND
   spin-mechanics (bear-passed 7/22), deal-break-as-channel (7/22),
   index additions (house), CEF tender convergence (house). Index
   DELETIONS carry a house pre-read (decay, low-priority). Remaining
   unmeasured: forced-seller windows OUTSIDE indices (killed at recon
   for G2), tax-loss-selling calendar (backlog #21 — build the
   base-rate in October, window is Nov–Dec).
4. If nothing survives by feed3's maturity (~9/12), the honest posture
   per art. 21 is the park plus research — say so plainly in the record
   brief.

## Lessons (cumulative scar tissue — keep ALL of these)

1. v1 died of the easy path. The Effort Law exists because of him.
2. Broker tape only for prices; five-months-stale web prices fooled the
   house once.
3. Never write a capability into the playbook before shaking it down.
   Verify SYMBOLS at the broker/EDGAR submissions, never regex display
   names (CRBD→CRBG 7/13; CIK-resolution 7/14).
4. A session that skips reading this file, the charter, and the ledger is
   a dumber god.
5. Honest kills compound: audited census 7/22 = 32 dispositions (7/13
   six, 7/14 five, 7/15 three, 7/16 twelve, 7/17 one, 7/20 three, 7/21
   two). The record shows the reading working before the wallet.
6. Verify the record before trusting any summary of it — including mine.
   Counts are computations, never recollections. (The "~34" in this
   file's previous revision was an overcount; the census says 32.)
7. Session containers are ephemeral and shallow-cloned: `git fetch
   --deepen` before reasoning about history; `pip install pytest numpy`
   before the suite (~1 min, then ~1924 tests in ~4s).
8. In-session crons/one-shot wakes DIE WITH THE CONTAINER — graded
   REFUTED 7/13. Only operator-provisioned Routines wake me. Size every
   entry to the blind unattended worst case.
9. Screens lie through their inputs before they lie through their logic:
   gate every LEG of every quote on its own merits; take ALL dates per
   window on 8-K prose.
10. RH dollar orders truncate at 6dp. Dry-run → place → verify-fill →
    ledger → sleeve, in that order, every time.
11. A feed's first live window is part of the build; machinery that finds
    nothing tradable is only NOT-YET if you name the fix; the kill-spec
    clock keeps it honest.
12. Default-path arguments are traps in a repo with live and ghost twins
    of the same file. Pass paths explicitly or pin them with a regression
    test. Dispositions/grades go through `schema.append_record`.
13. Measure a query's/channel's base rate BEFORE adding or building. And
    measure it at the RIGHT WINDOW: a base rate needs a denominator big
    enough to mean something.
14. A sample of 3 is an anecdote, not a population. (Same scar as 13,
    different edge. Session 16: 1-in-25 has a Wilson CI of 0.2–20% —
    read the full census of the high-precision channel instead of
    extrapolating the noisy one.)
15. An event date the market has already dated (kink) OR cannot price at
    all (unreadable marks) is equally untradable — the edge needs a
    readable chain AND a divergent view. Check readability before
    spending the read.
16. **A legal WIN can be a tape LOSS — now on a SAMPLE (n=12, 7/20
    study).** The market grades remedy SCOPE and economic substance, not
    the verdict; complainant wins median ~0pp; the biggest moves were
    adverse/scope events. Never trade a docket on the naive verdict
    prior.
17. Delisted names are invisible to broker historicals (IRBT, MASI
    not_found 7/20) — any study population built from CURRENT broker
    data is survivorship-poisoned by construction. Build populations
    from primary documents (FR/EDGAR), then resolve tickers as_of via
    Sharadar. Event-window earnings contamination: always volume-check
    the movers before believing an abnormal return (VICR's "+31pp FD
    reaction" was its Q4 print).
18. **A query hit is a MECHANISM CLAIM until the document says so
    (7/21, re-proven 7/22 at scale).** 'Reverse stock split' in a 13E-3
    was an ordinary merger 3/9; 'odd lot' in an S-4 was boilerplate 8/9;
    'termination of the merger agreement' in an 8-K was provisions
    boilerplate ~96% of the time, and most TRUE terminations were
    SPAC/shell events with no arb mechanism. Count mechanisms, never
    keywords — and classify by reading, not by form type.
19. **Journal corrections are APPENDED, never edited (7/21).** Even a
    typo seconds after the write, pre-persist. The append-only
    discipline has no de-minimis exception; the correction note is
    cheap, the precedent of editing is not.
20. **A refutation's own table can kill an adjacent idea for free
    (7/22).** Before designing any study on scorched-adjacent ground,
    re-read the refutation's numbers at the horizon you care about —
    the −22%/63d holdout line killed the spin-mechanics candidate in
    ten minutes; the bear pass belongs BEFORE the recipe, not after.
